scenarios.description: wrap(Writer) uses custom stringWidth that forces tooLong on short words and triggers writeLine then broken-word hyphenation path
scenarios.expectedResult: word 'ab' broken with hyphen then 'cd' on new line
pseudocode: TEST TC16 custom stringWidth triggers early wrap

scenarios.description: wrap(Writer) with includeExtraWordChars treats punctuation '*' as word character and keeps it attached
scenarios.expectedResult: input '*a* b' remains '*a*' on first line and 'b' second

scenarios.description: wrap(Writer) with excludeExtraWordChars treats '.' as separator and exercises punctuation branch resetting previousWasPunctuation
scenarios.expectedResult: input 'a.b' remains 'a.' then 'b' without trimming leading punctuation

scenarios.description: wrap(Writer) propagates IORuntimeException when Reader.close() throws IOException in finally
scenarios.expectedResult: IORuntimeException is thrown wrapping the close IOException
pseudocode: TEST TC19 reader.close throws wrapped IORuntimeException
public int read(char[] cbuf, int off, int len) { return -1; }
public void close() throws IOException { throw new IOException("fail-close"); }
